title: X/Twitter Platform Research and Strategy
description: Full platform research for X/Twitter covering algorithm mechanics, audience, competitors, and content format recommendations. Research only — no execution.

scope.in: Algorithm mechanics, audience demographics, competitor analysis (5+ AI tools), content format recommendations, effort estimate, go/no-go with trigger conditions.
scope.out: No draft posts, no content calendar, no execution.

dod.criterion: Research document covers X/Twitter algorithm mechanics with specific data
Competitor analysis maps at least 5 open-source AI tools' X presence
Content format recommendations specify what works for dev tools on X
Go/no-go verdict includes specific trigger conditions for when to start X execution

tasks.title: X/Twitter Algorithm Mechanics and Audience Research
tasks.user_scenario: CEO reads a clear breakdown of how X's algorithm works for dev tool content and who the target audience is.
tasks.scope: Research algorithm mechanics (link penalty, engagement signal hierarchy), audience demographics for AI/developer community, content format performance (threads vs single tweets vs image posts vs video). Save to x-research.md.

dod.criterion: Algorithm mechanics section documents link penalty percentage with at least 2 cited sources, reply/repost/bookmark weighting, and For You feed ranking
Audience section identifies AI/developer segment on X with specific accounts or communities as evidence
Content format section compares threads vs single tweets vs image posts vs video with performance data specific to developer/tech content
All claims include source attribution (URL, date, or named source) — no unsourced statistics

tasks.title: Open-Source AI Tool Competitor Presence on X/Twitter
tasks.user_scenario: CEO can see how 5+ comparable AI tools use X and what works vs what doesn't.
tasks.scope: Map 5+ open-source AI tool X accounts (CrewAI, LangChain, AutoGen, Cursor, Continue.dev, Aider, etc.). For each: follower count, posting frequency, engagement patterns, content mix, growth trajectory. Identify fast-grower tactics. Append to x-research.md.

dod.criterion: At least 5 open-source AI tool X accounts analyzed with follower count, posting frequency, and engagement rate
Each competitor entry identifies their highest and lowest performing content types with specific examples
Analysis includes at least one fast-grower account with explanation of what tactics worked
Patterns section synthesizes what works across all analyzed accounts

tasks.title: Effort Estimate and Go/No-Go Recommendation
tasks.user_scenario: CEO reads a 60-second executive summary and knows whether to invest in X now, later, or never, with clear trigger conditions.
tasks.scope: Synthesize findings into: minimally viable X presence, effort estimate (hours/week), timeline to meaningful engagement, go/no-go verdict, trigger conditions. Add executive summary to top of x-research.md.

dod.criterion: Effort estimate specifies hours/week for content creation, hours/week for engagement, and minimum commitment duration
Go/no-go verdict is a clear YES, NO, or NOT YET — not 'it depends' — with 2-3 sentences justifying
Trigger conditions section lists 3-5 specific, measurable conditions that would change the verdict
Executive summary at top of x-research.md readable in 60 seconds with verdict, key numbers, and next action
